title: Brownfield Access Review Domain Model
prompt: Read `./access-review-modernization-requirements.md`. Work only from that file. Do not ask follow-up questions. If you need assumptions, state them briefly and continue. Produce the domain-model artifact for this brownfield modernization slice. Identify the core entities, authoritative relationships, bounded contexts only when justified, and a ubiquitous-language glossary that keeps legacy or tenant-specific compatibility terms visible without letting them silently become the canonical future model.

assertions.name: core-entities-are-explicit
assertions.description: The output identifies business entities and relationships instead of implementation objects.

assertions.name: ubiquitous-language-is-resolved
assertions.description: Ambiguous or overlapping terms are normalized into a stable glossary.

assertions.name: brownfield-boundaries-stay-visible
assertions.description: Legacy-only or compatibility-only concepts remain explicit.

assertions.name: bounded-contexts-are-justified
assertions.description: Contexts are introduced only when the slice actually requires them.

assertions.name: prepares-downstream-handoff
assertions.description: The resulting artifact can feed spec writing or architecture work directly.
